body:
This PR:
 - Moves the edmf coriolis force out of `sgs_flux_tendency!` and into it's own tendency file
 - Removes the edmf coriolis force's dependence on TC-internals
 - Deletes `initialize_forcing` 🎉
title: Unify edmf coriolis

body: #992 changed how `w` for updrafts is accessed, which needs to be reflected in the mse tables. This PR fixes it. Found in #1025
title: Fix mse tables

body:
This PR refactors the precipitation:
 - Removes unused variables
 - Makes some precipitation fraction model functions not depend on the edmf model. The result is that `EDMF_Precipitation.jl` no longer depends on the edmf struct
title: Refactor precipitation

body:
This PR
 - removes some places where `real_face_indices` is used
 - Removes `real_face_indices` itself now that it's no longer needed
 - Deletes the truncated GCM mesh, in favor of using https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCore.jl/pull/821
title: Remove `real_face_indices`

body:
This PR will close #994 

## Purpose 
Integrate the use of `ClimaCoreSpectra` for spectra calculation in ClimaAtmos. 

Will close #994 .



## Content
Add spectrum calculations in the AMIP driver/postprocessing pipeline. The test case used for initial integration example is moist baroclinic wave. The intent is to replicate the existing behavior in [ClimateMachine](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimateMachine.jl/blob/master/src/Diagnostics/atmos_gcm_spectra.jl#L88) 
- Solution implemented as in the ClimateMachine implementation.

Review checklist

I have:
- [x] followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- [x] followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- [x] followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- [x] checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.
title: Integrate spectra calculator for moist baro wave ex

body: This PR adds `ClimaCoreSpectra` to the test environment, see [this comment](https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaAtmos.jl/pull/1014#discussion_r1005959541)
title: Add ClimaCoreSpectra to test env

body:
This PR refactors the reference state computations by:
 - splitting out the ode solution from the variable assignment, this allows us to do this once before we enter the bycolumn loop, and we can cache the solution, too, in case we want to re-compute the variables later (e.g., for the consistency test)
 - Remove use of TC internals in the ref state computations
 - Move the ref state computations into src/

This may help make initialization on a sphere much faster, and it may also just help us generalize how initialization works.
title: Relax assumptions in ref_state
